seed: quiet vs pressure; symmetry_vs_break; diagonal_argument; counterflow; shatter against tide; hard_edges; sharp_vs_soft; island; clarity_confusion | abstract variation request 100; target batch count 500
title: Tide of Tension
reasoning: The emotional tone of this artwork reflects the duality of quiet and pressure, capturing a symphony of contrasts. The 'quiet' elements are symbolized by faint peach and off-white hues, setting a soft foundation that represents calmness and subtlety. In contrast, the rich dark navy and compressed red serve to evoke tension and energy, creating an atmosphere that oscillates between serenity and the weight of pressure. The layout, influenced by a broken frame, establishes a sense of restriction at the edges where the dark colors congregate, while the lighter colors tend to flow towards the center, suggesting a release from pressure. This dynamic interaction conveys a visual narrative of tension and relaxation, enriched by deliberate disruptions from broken pixels. The overall composition remains abstract and avoids literal interpretations, focusing instead on the interplay of colors and emotions.

layout.name: broken_frame
layout.description: A border nearly encloses the square but has deliberate breaks where emotion leaks through.

composition.name: pressure_vs_release
composition.idea: Dense pixels collect at one edge and loosen into air across the square.

referenceExample.title: Lonely Signal
referenceExample.pattern: single bright dot with faint surrounding echo

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
